sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.sectionTitle: History is a minimum requirements major. Students may declare the major as soon as they have met the prerequisites listed above. There is no application process. To declare the major, please make an appointment with History Undergraduate Advising .

sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.rawLine: The choice of remaining elective History coursework (to reach a total of 60 credits) is entirely at the student's discretion. This coursework varies, however, depending on whether you have chosen to declare one of the History Thematic Majors . For the Thematic Majors, students must choose 25 elective credits from the approved course lists available by clicking here . Remaining elective credit to reach 60 total History credits is at the student's discretion.

sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.sectionTitle: The following is a list of coursework required as prerequisites for UW Seattle Master in Teaching Secondary Teacher Education Program candidates who wish to earn a history endorsement upon completion of the MIT. History pedagogy will be covered in the College of Education programming. For more information, or to arrange a transcript review in advance of your application deadline, please visit Department of History Advising . Transcripts, course descriptions and syllabi may all be required for a complete transcript review. The transcript evaluation process can take some time, so please email unofficial transcripts and other course information to histadv@uw.edu no later than two weeks before the application deadline.

sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.sectionTitle: The following is a list of coursework required as prerequisites for the UW Seattle Master in Teaching Secondary Teacher Education Program candidates who wish to earn a social studies endorsement upon completion of the MIT. Social studies pedagogy will be covered in the College of Education programming. For more information, or to arrange a transcript review in advance of your application deadline, please visit Department of History Advising . Transcripts, course descriptions and syllabi may all be required for a complete transcript review. The transcript evaluation process can take some time, so please email unofficial transcripts and other course information to histadv@uw.edu no later than two weeks before the application deadline.

requirementCueLines: -10 credits of college history with 2.50 average.
-10 credits of composition or writing with a 2.00 in each course.
10 credits of approved courses in the Modern period.
10 credits of approved courses in the Pre-Modern period.

30 credits of upper division history coursework (300-400 level) completed in residence at the University of Washington. Transfer and extension credits are not classified as "in residence." HSTRY 388, HSTRY 494, and HSTRY 498 courses all count as part of upper division history coursework.
5 credits each in four of the following six areas of study:
Civics: 10 credits/2 courses
Economics: 10 credits/2 courses
Geography: 5 credits/1 course
History Endorsement Requirements
History Endorsement Requirements | Department of History | University of Washington

History is a minimum requirements major. Students may declare the major as soon as they have met the prerequisites listed above. There is no application process. To declare the major, please make an appointment with History Undergraduate Advising .
History: 35 credits/7 courses
History: 50 credits/10 courses
If you have questions about these requirements, please contact the History Undergraduate Advising Office .
In order for a course to count toward these prerequisite requirements, a grade of 2.5 or its equivalent must be recorded.
Major Requirements
Middle Eastern & African History (all HSTAFM courses and HSTAM 325)
One 5-credit HSTRY 388: Introduction to History (Junior Seminar) completed at the University of Washington.

One 5-credit HSTRY 494: Historiography OR HSTRY 498: Colloquium in History (Senior Seminar) completed at the University of Washington in which a major research paper or its equivalent will be written.
One course (approx. 5 credits) in Historical Methodologies
One course (approx. 5 credits) in Historical Methodologies/Research
One course (approx. 5 credits) in Historical Research
One course (approx. 5 credits) in History of Colonial America and the Founding of the U.S.
One course (approx. 5 credits) in History of the Ancient World
One course (approx. 5 credits) in History of the Early Modern & Modern World
One course (approx. 5 credits) in History of the Medieval World through 1450
One course (approx. 5 credits) in History of the United States in the Long Twentieth Century
One course (approx. 5 credits) in History of Washington State and the Pacific Northwest
One course (approx. 5 credits) in Human Geography
One course (approx. 5 credits) in Institutions and Politics in American Government
One course (approx. 5 credits) in International Relations, Politics or Globalization
One course (approx. 5 credits) in Macroeconomics
One course (approx. 5 credits) in Microeconomics

Please note: Undergraduates at the University of Washington who began their endorsement prerequisite coursework prior to Winter 2018 may use the "old" (pre-2018) guidelines instead of these. This policy is in recognition of work they may have already done according to old guidelines; no other candidates may use old endorsement guidelines.
Social Studies Endorsement Requirements
Social Studies Endorsement Requirements | Department of History | University of Washington

Students must complete a Graduation Application no later than the third Friday of the quarter in which they plan to graduate. Students must make an appointment with a History adviser to complete this paperwork. Graduation applications may be completed up to two quarters in advance.
Students who declared the History major prior to Autumn 2014 have a different set of requirements. Contact History Undergraduate Advising with questions.

The choice of remaining elective History coursework (to reach a total of 60 credits) is entirely at the student's discretion. This coursework varies, however, depending on whether you have chosen to declare one of the History Thematic Majors . For the Thematic Majors, students must choose 25 elective credits from the approved course lists available by clicking here . Remaining elective credit to reach 60 total History credits is at the student's discretion.
chooseStatements: The choice of remaining elective History coursework (to reach a total of 60 credits) is entirely at the student's discretion. This coursework varies, however, depending on whether you have chosen to declare one of the History Thematic Majors . For the Thematic Majors, students must choose 25 elective credits from the approved course lists available by clicking here . Remaining elective credit to reach 60 total History credits is at the student's discretion.

parsedRequirementAtomCandidates.title: HSTRY 388
sourceLineHints: One 5-credit HSTRY 388: Introduction to History (Junior Seminar) completed at the University of Washington.

This course must be completed before a student may take the senior seminar; concurrent enrollment is not permitted. Students should take at least two upper division history courses before they take their HSTRY 388.
parsedRequirementAtomCandidates.sourceSectionReason: course row without prerequisite-only or course-list section cues

parsedRequirementAtomCandidates.title: HSTRY 494
sourceLineHints: One 5-credit HSTRY 494: Historiography OR HSTRY 498: Colloquium in History (Senior Seminar) completed at the University of Washington in which a major research paper or its equivalent will be written.
parsedRequirementAtomCandidates.sourceSectionReason: course row without prerequisite-only or course-list section cues

parsedDegreeMapBlockCandidates.title: History parsed official source requirements
sourceLineHints: -10 credits of college history with 2.50 average.
-10 credits of composition or writing with a 2.00 in each course.
10 credits of approved courses in the Modern period.
10 credits of approved courses in the Pre-Modern period.

30 credits of upper division history coursework (300-400 level) completed in residence at the University of Washington. Transfer and extension credits are not classified as "in residence." HSTRY 388, HSTRY 494, and HSTRY 498 courses all count as part of upper division history coursework.
5 credits each in four of the following six areas of study:

parsedRequirementGroups.label: The choice of remaining elective History coursework (to reach a total of 60 credits) is entirely at the student's discretion. This coursework varies, however, depending on whether you have chosen to declare one of the History Thematic Majors . For the Thematic Majors, students must choose 25 elective credits from the approved course lists available by clicking here . Remaining elective credit to reach 60 total History credits is at the student's discretion.
